risks.description: Material is partially legible as MS - 12.20 with optional MS - 12.10; interpreted as mild/carbon steel, but exact grade and casting alloy standard are not confirmed.
risks.mitigation: Confirm material grade and revision before release; proceed provisionally as machinable mild/carbon steel.

risks.description: The part is specified as a one-piece cast body with casting-defect and flash-trim requirements, while the shop profile does not list in-house foundry/casting capability.
risks.mitigation: Use an approved outside casting/foundry supplier, then perform machining and inspection in-house where capable.

risks.description: Datum-based GD&T and general profile requirements may require CMM/FAI; the shop profile states CMM is not in-house.
risks.mitigation: Use outside CMM FAI for first article or critical datum/profile verification; use in-house gauges for production checks where acceptable.

risks.description: Datum B/C definitions, number of final bores, and the status of F007 and F010 are partially legible or low-confidence.
risks.mitigation: Obtain drawing clarification before final quoting or process release; use extracted defaults only for provisional feasibility.

outside_processes_needed.reason: One-piece cast component body is required, but no in-house foundry/casting process is listed.
Datum-based position, perpendicularity, flatness, and profile verification may need CMM capability; profile lists cmm_in_house as false.

feasibility.assessment_notes: The part envelope 234.88 x 149.94 x 55 mm fits the listed VMC travel of 600 x 400 x 400 mm. Machining tolerances are no tighter than 0.1 mm GD&T / 0.2 mm diameter total tolerance, which are within the listed careful machining capability of 0.02 mm, but casting procurement and CMM inspection are risks.

clarifications.question: Confirm the material specification 'MS - 12.20, OPT: MS - 12.10' and whether it is a cast mild/carbon steel grade acceptable to the shop and foundry.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Material grade confirmation is required for casting procurement, machinability, and compliance.
clarifications.suggested_default: Proceed provisionally as machinable mild/carbon steel until confirmed.

clarifications.question: Confirm datum feature definitions for B and C and the exact feature-control frames for the Ø9 holes and Bore R.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Datum interpretation affects machining setup and GD&T inspection feasibility.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use datum A as bottom face, datum B as right Ø9 hole axis, and datum C as left Ø9 hole axis.

clarifications.question: Confirm whether there are two or three final Ø31.10/30.90 socket bores and whether the 4X chamfer note applies only to Bore R.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Feature count and chamfer scope affect machining and inspection requirements.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use two Section A-A bores plus Bore R; apply 4X chamfers to Bore R only.

clarifications.question: Confirm the through depths/axial lengths for the Ø31.10/30.90 bores and Ø9.00 holes.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Depth associations are moderately ambiguous in the extraction.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use 15.0 mm for large bores and 17.5 mm for Ø9 holes/lugs.

clarifications.question: Clarify whether the Ø28.94 I/P socket-clearance and Ø10.25/9.85 counterbore/clearance features are final part requirements.
clarifications.why_it_matters: These features are physically machinable but their final applicability is uncertain.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat Ø28.94 as in-process and Ø10.25/9.85 as low-confidence counterbore until verified.

assumptions.text: MS is interpreted as machinable mild/carbon steel because the material profile lists common carbon/alloy steels and no hardness or exotic material condition is specified.
Missing casting/foundry capability can be handled through an outside supplier because the routing policy does not require all processes in-house and an outside vendor placeholder exists.
The shop can use outside CMM FAI for datum/profile verification where in-house standard-room inspection is insufficient.

reasoning_summary: This irregular cast steel component fits the available VMC machining envelope, and the extracted machined tolerances/GD&T are within the shop’s listed careful machining capability. The part is feasible to proceed provisionally, but with medium risk because the shop does not list in-house casting, the material grade is partially legible, several datum/feature callouts are ambiguous, and CMM FAI may need to be outsourced.
